title: US Judge orders removal of Trump's name from Kennedy Center; president fumes
content: A US federal judge has ordered the Trump administration to remove President Trump's name from the Kennedy Center. The court ruled that renaming the Washington institution, a memorial to John F. Kennedy, requires congressional approval. The judge mandated the removal of all Trump signage and references within two weeks, following a board decision that faced significant backlash.

title: 'It ends up...': Sundar Pichai on Google's answer to Anthropic & OpenAI's AI coding agents
content: Google's CEO Sundar Pichai acknowledges a current lag in AI coding agents. He states competitors like Anthropic lead in complex developer tasks. Pichai expresses strong confidence that internal data and new model iterations will rapidly bridge this gap. Google is actively working to regain its position at the forefront of this crucial technology.

title: Shubman Gill’s stunning century powers GT into IPL 2026 final against RCB
content: Gujarat Titans reached their third IPL final after defeating Rajasthan Royals. Shubman Gill scored a magnificent century to guide Gujarat in their chase. Rajasthan had posted a strong total thanks to Vaibhav Sooryavanshi's brilliant knock. Gujarat's opening partnership was key to their success. They will now face Royal Challengers Bengaluru in the final.

title: Four lines that 'layoff email' from Meta Leadership has for those on H-1B visa
content: Meta has initiated another round of significant layoffs, impacting nearly 8,000 employees globally, with notifications starting in Singapore. While many are let go, over 7,000 will transition to AI initiatives. The company is providing immigration guidance and severance packages, including 16 weeks' pay plus two weeks per service year, and 18 months of COBRA coverage for affected staff.

title: ‘Have to tape his hand’: Gill’s hilarious reaction to Sai's hit-wicket dismissal
content: Gujarat Titans captain Shubman Gill spoke about Sai Sudharsan's unusual back-to-back hit-wicket dismissals.  Despite this, Gill and Sudharsan's record partnership powered Gujarat Titans to a massive win.  Gill's century and Sudharsan's quickfire fifty secured their place in the IPL 2026 final.  The team's strong understanding was a key factor in their success.

title: Kaal Bhairava goes to Europe: Why Portugal deal is gamechanger for India’s defence sector
content: An Indian-designed autonomous combat aircraft entering the European manufacturing ecosystem is an endeavour Indian defence planners seemingly wanted for years. But beyond the symbolism lies a far bigger story involving military technology, Nato interoperability, artificial intelligence, export controls, defence geopolitics and the rapidly changing nature of modern warfare. The agreement also highlights something increasingly visible in global defence manufacturing as the military power is no longer built entirely inside geographical limits.

title: CEO Mark Zuckerberg's goodbye memo to 8k fired Meta staff has 2 promises for survivors
content: Mark Zuckerberg's goodbye memo to 8,000 fired Meta employees on May 20 doubled as a message to the 70,000 staff still standing. The Meta CEO made two promises: no further company-wide layoffs in 2026, and better communication after weeks of internal anxiety. The memo lands as Meta funnels $125-145 billion into AI infrastructure, shifts 7,000 staff to AI projects, and cancels 6,000 open roles.

title: Sikh PIO's murder conviction triggers storm over carrying kirpan in public
content: A British Sikh man was convicted of murder using a kirpan, sparking calls to ban the ceremonial dagger in public. Accusations of two-tier policing arose after officers handcuffed the dying victim, believing a false claim of racial abuse. The victim's mother was convicted of aiding the killer. Investigations into police conduct are underway.

title: Blake Lively stays silent as Taylor Swift-Travis Kelce wedding nears
content: Taylor Swift and Travis Kelce seem to be trying their best to keep things private as they gear up to get married in New York City amid the pop star’s strained bond with Blake Lively, just weeks before tying the knot. While the billionaire artist has maintained silence and has remained publicly distant from her former best friend, Blake Lively, it seems the actress is focused on improving her image.

title: Who Is Lauren Fryer? Meet Declan Rice’s longtime partner before World Cup 2026
content: Arsenal star midfielder Declan Rice has been with his girlfriend, Lauren Fryer, for over a decade now even as the two have continued to get trolled for their relationship in public for the last few years. While Lauren Fryer has remained pretty private about her life, Declan Rice has often defended her in public as he spoke about how she has changed his life for the better.

title: Browns, Raiders linked to blockbuster trade for 49ers’ $120 million NFL Star
content: The San Francisco 49ers’ star player, Brandon Aiyuk, has been experiencing a pretty challenging time in the last few years as he was placed on PUP in the last season after sustaining brutal injuries in Week 7 in 2024 sparking major concer in the team. Though the team seems focused on trading him and the Washington Commanders have been strongly linked to Brandon Aiyuk, nothing has been confirmed yet. Amid this, the Las Vegas Raiders and the Cleveland Browns have emerged as strong landing spots for him.

title: IPL 2026 Orange Cap & Purple Cap: Sooryavanshi stays on top, Rabada surges ahead
content: The IPL 2026 Orange and Purple Cap races are set for a thrilling conclusion. Vaibhav Sooryavanshi leads the Orange Cap race, but Shubman Gill and Sai Sudharsan are close behind. Kagiso Rabada has taken the lead in the Purple Cap standings. The final match will decide the tournament's top performers.

title: Major shock! After Sinner, Djokovic knocked out of French Open 2026
content: French Open 2026 witnessed two huge upsets as Novak Djokovic and Jannik Sinner crashed out of the tournament. Teenager Joao Fonseca stunned Djokovic in a five-set thriller after coming back from two sets down. Earlier, World No. 1 Sinner collapsed physically in extreme Paris heat and lost to Juan Manuel Cerundolo, ending his 30-match winning streak and opening up the men’s draw.

title: Twisha Sharma’s ashes immersed in Rishikesh; family recalls her deep bond with Ganga river
content: Twisha Sharma's ashes were immersed in the Ganga at Rishikesh, a place she deeply cherished. Her family is determined to pursue justice in the high-profile Bhopal case, acknowledging it's an early stage of a significant struggle against powerful individuals. They expressed hope for government and judicial intervention to set a precedent.

title: 'He doesn't slog': Parag lauds Sooryavanshi's 96-run knock despite RR loss
content: Riyan Parag praised Vaibhav Sooryavanshi after Rajasthan Royals’ IPL 2026 exit, saying the 15-year-old “doesn't slog” and instead “calculates and plays his shots”. Sooryavanshi smashed 96 off 47 balls, but Gujarat Titans chased down 215 comfortably thanks to Shubman Gill’s 104 and Sai Sudharsan’s 58. Gujarat reached their third IPL final and will face RCB in Ahmedabad.

title: Sidda meets Cong high command in Delhi a day after resignation - what’s on his 'wishlist'
content: Outgoing Karnataka CM Siddaramaiah met with Congress leaders in Delhi, fulfilling his promise to resign and paving the way for a transition. He reportedly sought cabinet berths for his son and loyalists, while the party prepares for a state unit reorganization where he is expected to play a key role.

title: What led social media influencer Ronisha Satti to attempt suicide? Inside the feud & legal battle
content: An Instagram influencer attempted suicide after accusing her former fiancé of online harassment and defamation, including sharing intimate videos. The fiancé's legal team denies the allegations, claiming the influencer was not legally divorced and had defrauded his client. Both parties have filed police complaints against each other.

title: Operation Sheruwali enters Day 7; searches continue in J&K’s Rajouri
content: Security forces are in the seventh day of a relentless hunt for two to three terrorists in Rajouri's dense forests. The joint operation, bolstered by helicopters, drones, and sniffer dogs, intensified with heavy firing and shelling. A terrorist hideout was also discovered and destroyed. Top officers are overseeing the critical anti-terror mission.

title: Sai Sudharsan scripts history, joins Virat Kohli & Chris Gayle in elite IPL club
content: Sai Sudharsan achieved a remarkable feat in IPL 2026 Qualifier 2, joining Virat Kohli and Chris Gayle by scoring 700+ runs for the second consecutive season. He also equaled David Warner's nine 50-plus scores in a single IPL edition. Sudharsan and Shubman Gill's monumental opening stand set new IPL playoff partnership records.

title: How government aims to save billions in forex with ethanol blended petrol
content: Ethanol-blended petrol is made by mixing ethanol with petrol. Ethanol is a biofuel that is derived from a wide variety of food crops and feed stocks like sugarcane and petrol. Under the Ethanol Blended Petrol Programme, the original target for achieving E20 petrol sales.

title: A Note of Discord: US divided on proposed Trump $250 bill
content: America is considering a $250 bill featuring Donald Trump's face for its 250th birthday, a move that bypasses a law against living presidents on currency. Designs are prepared, sparking debate and satire. This proposal, alongside a UFC event on Trump's birthday, highlights the nation's deep divisions as its semiquincentennial approaches.

title: Shubman Gill and Sai Sudharsan script T20 history, become first-ever pair to ...
content: Shubman Gill and Sai Sudharsan created T20 history during Gujarat Titans’ IPL 2026 Qualifier 2 clash against Rajasthan Royals by becoming the first men’s pair to register 11 century partnerships. The duo achieved the feat in just 48 innings, surpassing legendary combinations like Chris Gayle-Virat Kohli and Babar Azam-Mohammad Rizwan while chasing 215 in Mullanpur.

title: Ranveer Singh tried to patch up after Don 3 fallout, Akhtars refused: Report
content: The reported fallout between filmmaker Farhan Akhtar and actor Ranveer Singh following the latter’s exit from Don 3 has reportedly escalated further. According to an insider close to the matter, Ranveer Singh allegedly attempted to smooth over tensions by offering to collaborate on another film with either Farhan Akhtar or his sister Zoya Akhtar. However, the insider claimed that the proposal was turned down immediately.

title: Eliminating language, culture & faith: Xi's repression of Uyghurs enters new phase
content: China's Xinjiang crackdown shifts focus from camps to identity, language, and religion. Despite claims of re-education graduates, a vast network of prisons, surveillance, and boarding schools persists. Uyghur culture faces suppression through education and labor programs, sparking renewed international concern over alleged crimes against humanity.

title: 16-year-old student found hanging in Lucknow hostel; suicide note found
content: A 16-year-old student was found dead in a Lucknow hostel, with police investigating after her family allegedly tried to move the body for cremation without informing authorities. A note reading “I quit” was found in her room, considered a potential suicide note. The post-mortem and forensic reports are awaited.

title: Suspense over Vinesh Phogat’s weight category ahead of Asian Games trials
content: Wrestler Vinesh Phogat's participation in Asian Games trials is confirmed by courts, but her weight category remains a dispute. While Phogat desires to compete in 53kg, the WFI insists on 50kg, citing her "Iconic Athlete" status linked to her previous 50kg Olympic campaign. The final decision on her category is pending.

title: SP woman leader dragged by hair, 'beaten' inside house in UP; CCTV captures chilling attack
content: A Samajwadi Party leader in Chandauli has been brutally attacked inside her home. Gargi Singh Patel alleges her business associate's family assaulted her with iron rods. She claims the attack was part of a political conspiracy to harm her career. CCTV footage reportedly captured the incident. Police are investigating the assault and the alleged political links.

title: Sooryavanshi equals Maxwell’s 12-year-old IPL record with stunning 96-run blitz
content: Teenage sensation Vaibhav Sooryavanshi smashed 96 off 47 balls in IPL 2026 Qualifier 2 against Gujarat Titans, narrowly missing a century for the second consecutive knockout game. The 15-year-old equalled Glenn Maxwell's record for most dismissals in the 90s in a season and became the fastest to 1000 runs by balls faced.

title: CBSE OSM row: 20 answer-sheet mix-ups detected; over 13K copies evaluated manually - report
content: Central Board of Secondary Education's new digital evaluation system encountered problems. Around 20 answer sheets were mixed up. Over 13,000 required manual checking due to poor scanning. This led to a delay in the post-result verification portal. The board is working to fix technical glitches and ensure a smooth process for students.

title: Why the Strait of Hormuz worries China more than Malacca
content: A new report highlights China's key maritime vulnerability lies at the Strait of Hormuz, not the Malacca Strait. This shifts strategic competition in the Indian Ocean to a new arena involving India, France, and the US. China's energy imports through this region expose it to potential exploitation by rival powers.

title: Vaibhav Sooryavanshi creates IPL history, becomes fastest ever to...
content: Rajasthan Royals' Vaibhav Sooryavanshi shattered records in Qualifier 2, becoming the fastest to 1000 IPL runs, surpassing Andre Russell.  Despite early wickets and a teammate's injury, the teenager's explosive 96 off 47 balls, featuring eight fours and seven sixes, propelled RR's innings and added to his historic IPL 2026 campaign.

title: ‘Everyone is equal before God’: VIP darshan in temples wrong & discriminatory, says Madras HC
content: The bench rejected a submission made by the state’s additional advocate-general, P V Balasubramaniam, that withdrawing paid VIP darshan would cause revenue loss to the temples.

title: 'Referring wides in cricket needs to stop': Kevin Pietersen sparks fresh IPL debate
content: Former England captain Kevin Pietersen sparked debate during IPL 2026 Qualifier 2 after criticising wide-ball referrals in cricket. “Referring wides in cricket needs to stop,” Pietersen posted on X while watching Gujarat Titans vs Rajasthan Royals. His remark divided fans, with some saying reviews slow down T20 games, while others defended technology for ensuring accurate decisions in high-pressure matches.

title: Uttar Pradesh emphasised to prevent water-borne diseases in rural areas through community driven participation
content: Mr. Shekhar, a 2004 batch Indian Administrative Service (IAS) officer, added that the women associated with Field Testing work are being paid honorarium according to the prescribed rates for the testing work carried out by them

content: The petition highlighting the lack of laws and protective mechanisms for young girls trafficked into prostitution was filed by the non-governmental organisation (NGO) Prajwala in 2004

content: Opposition leader questions how TCS lost CBSE contract to COEMPT, which had a poor past track record.

title: Delhi High Court upholds TRAI regulations capping television ads to 12 minutes per hour

content: Minister for Industries S. Keerthana says the reform aims to further formalise the sector through specific and targeted measures that address multiple needs of all stakeholders

content: Unscheduled power cuts are affecting household appliances, and irrigation activities in farmlands amid cropping season, say residents of Parameswaramangalam village

title: Woman ‘murdered’ by paramour in Eluru; body exhumed

content: Metering will not be introduced for agricultural consumers under the proposed model, says TGRPDCL Chairman and Managing Director Musharraf Ali Faruqui; the existing Telangana discoms were under severe financial stress, with accumulated losses reaching ₹69,741 crore by FY 2025 with the government departments alone reportedly owing ₹54,090 crore in outstanding arrears

title: Kerala’s Pariyathukavu residents hope government intervention will put an end to eviction threat
content: Pariyathukavu, a settlement in Ernakulam district, occupied by seven Dalit families for decades, has become the epicentre of a socio-political controversy with attempts to evict the residents, who have been engaged in a prolonged legal battle to protect their homes. G. Ragesh visits the area to gauge the gravity of the situation as both the residents and the claimants of the land await anxiously for finality

title: CCMB scientists identify rare gene behind Indian girl’s red hair
content: From this, researchers have provided fresh insight into the country’s complex genetic landscape by uncovering the role of the MC1R gene (melanocortin 1 receptor), a key regulator of skin and hair pigmentation.

content: A KIIFB director board meeting held on Friday decided to keep the agenda in abeyance so as to allow the government to decide its policy with respect to the institution

content: Sources said supporters of Mr. Shanmugam, at a meeting, had urged him to continue working within the AIADMK

content: Temperatures may continue to hover around 45°C to 46°C on Saturday in several districts, while the IMD forecasts heavy rain, thunderstorms and strong winds over South Coastal Andhra Pradesh and Rayalaseema during the weekend

title: Census in West Bengal to start from August 1: CM Suvendu
content: The Chief Minister said that West Bengal needs to catch up with other states in conduct of the census; he expressed concern over demographic changes and said census data will help in policy decisions

content: Home Minister said the government is planning to assign additional areas of responsibility to the BSF; noted that the most significant gap in the security grid lay in the incomplete fencing along the Bengal boundary

title: Naidu orders Zero FIR mechanism to curb cyber crime
content: Chief Minister directs immediate registration of complaints through proposed cyber war room to enable swift bank intervention, while unveiling a multi-pronged strategy to tackle online fraud and digital arrest scams

content: SC says there will be no further extension; rejects arguments that TET mandate is a retrospective implementation of RTE Act; says teachers’ service cannot come at the cost of children’s educational future

title: Operation to locate terrorists in forests of Rajouri enters seventh day
content: Soldiers are continuing their pursuit of a group of terrorists reported to be holed up in the forests of Rajouri, in the Pir Panjal Valley, for the seventh straight day on Friday

title: Rise in women misusing law to get lucrative settlements: SC
content: the supreme court flagged a "worrying trend" of women misusing laws on dowry harassment, domestic violence and Pocso . the court said genuine cases were getting overshadowed and obscured by the "overwhelming" number of false cases filed by spouses as an "arm-twisting" method to reach a more lucrative settlement .
